title: quercus × carrissoana
intro: Quercus × carrissoana is a hybrid plant, resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus faginea, and is commonly known as Portuguese oak. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Quercus × carrissoana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Quercus × carrissoana can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and experience its health benefits.
